title: Write a Brand Positioning Statement
tagline: Define target audience, category, key benefit, and reason to believe.
scenario: A new South African meal-kit delivery startup is about to launch. They deliver pre-portioned ingredients and recipes to working professionals in Johannesburg and Cape Town. They need to articulate who they are, who they serve, and why they are different.
brief: Write a brand positioning statement using the classic framework: "For [target audience] who [need], [brand] is the [category] that [key benefit] because [reason to believe]." Then defend each element.
deliverables: The positioning statement in one sentence
A defense of each element: why this target audience, why this category framing, why this benefit over alternatives
One alternative positioning you considered and rejected, with your reasoning
submissionGuidance: A positioning statement is not a tagline. It is an internal strategic document. It should be precise, not catchy.

rubric.label: Positioning Statement
rubric.description: Is the statement clear, specific, and following the classic framework?

rubric.label: Element Defense
rubric.description: Is each element justified with market or customer insight?

rubric.label: Alternative Analysis
rubric.description: Does the rejected alternative show genuine strategic thinking?
